title: EdU detection on tissue sections
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4hkf7","text":"This protocol describes the procedure for EdU detection used by the Seifert Lab. It assumes a starting sample of a paraffin-embedded tissue section and refers to the Seifert Lab protocol for fluorescent immunohistochemistry [add link when published].","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"301lu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8n158","text":"It is adapted from the procedure described in A. Salic, T.J. Mitchison, A chemical method for fast and sensitive detection of DNA synthesis in vivo, Proc. Natl. Acad. Sci. U.S.A.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":140,"length":7},{"style":"italic","offset":149,"length":29}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1f9r7","text":"105 (7) 2415-2420, https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.0712168105 (2008).","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":19,"length":39}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"947054AFB40C11EFB5E30A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.0712168105"}}}}
steps: Deparaffinize and rehydrate slides per normal IHC protocol
If using subsequent antibodies for IHC, perform antigen retrieval
Rinse slides two times in Tris-buffered saline (TBS), 1x solution Fisher Scientific Catalog #BP24721
Leave slides in TBS until reaction cocktail is ready
OPTIONAL Block for 00:05:00 to 00:10:00 in appropriate blocking solution This is not necessary for rodent ear or skin tissue

Prepare EdU reaction cocktail: A B C 1 Component Volume (μL) Notes 2 2 M Tris, pH 8.5 5 100 mL = 24.2 g 3 50 mM CuSO4 2 1 mL = 7.98 mg 4 0.5 M ascorbic acid 20 1 mL = 88 mg *UNSTABLE AT RT, MAKE FRESH 5 AlexaFluor Azide (0.25 mg/mL) 0.2 0.5 mg in 2 mL DMSO 6 ddH2O 73 7 8 Total Volume: 100 IMPORTANT : use the reaction cocktail within 00:15:00 of preparing Use half the indicated AlexaFluor Azide to reduce background staining (0.1 µL per 100 µL 10 mM EdU = 2.55 mg/mL too much ascorbic acid messes up phalloidin staining 250 nM AlexaFluor azide (AF488) for staining of live cells is 0.45 µL/mL Use half the indicated AlexaFluor Azide to reduce background staining (0.1 µL per 100 µL)
Add reaction cocktail to each slide 100 µL to 250 µL usually works well
Incubate for 00:30:00 at Room temperature protected from light
Wash slides two times for 00:05:00 each in TBS
EITHER: Continue with normal fluorescent IHC protocol from blocking step IMPORTANT : keep slides protected from light for all steps to reduce bleaching of EdU signal
OR : Counterstain nuclei by incubating with 1 μg/mL Hoechst 33342 Contributed by users Catalog #H3570 for 00:05:00
Mount with Prolong Gold Thermo Fisher Scientific Catalog #P36930
creator: Ashley Seifert
